lt.gd: This bill supplements or reduces appropriations otherwise provided for the support and operation of state government for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2025 and ending June 30, 2026 and appropriates funds for the support and operation of state government for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2026 and ending June 30, 2027.
lt.hp: This bill:provides appropriations for the use and support of certain state agencies;provides appropriations for other purposes as described; andprovides intent language.
lt.moni: Money Appropriated in this Bill:This bill appropriates ($90,397,300) in operating and capital budgets for fiscal year 2026, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $826,600 in business-like activities for fiscal year 2026, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $8,597,058,800 in operating and capital budgets for fiscal year 2027, including:$1,588,381,800 from General Fund; and$4,357,600 from Income Tax Fund; and$7,004,319,400 from various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $1,776,100 in expendable funds and accounts for fiscal year 2027, including:$303,000 from General Fund; and$1,473,100 from various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $5,237,200 in business-like activities for fiscal year 2027, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $146,879,100 in restricted fund and account transfers for fiscal year 2027, including:$53,379,200 from General Fund; and$93,499,900 from various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $221,493,900 in fiduciary funds for fiscal year 2027, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.
lt.oc: Other Special Clauses:This bill provides a special effective date.

leg.enact: Be it enacted by the Legislature of the state of Utah:

sectionText.summary: The following sums of money are appropriated for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2025, and ending June 30, 2026. These are additions to amounts previously appropriated for fiscal year 2026.

licat.summary: Under the terms and conditions of Title 63J, Chapter 1, Budgetary Procedures Act, the Legislature appropriates the following sums of money from the funds or accounts indicated for the use and support of the government of the state of Utah.

agency.name: Department of Health and Human Services
agency.display: DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES

lineitem.name: Operations
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Operations
lineitem.appintent: The Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report to the Social Services Appropriations Subcommittee by May 1st, 2026 on the following related to Attorney General representation costs for the Division of Child and Family Services : (1) Attorney General costs incurred and paid for by the Division in fiscal years 2021 through 2025 and the sources from which they were paid; (2) Historical requests by the Division during that same time period for supplemental appropriations and the reasons for the requests; (3) Analysis of what has driven over budget costs and how to better plan for those costs; (4) An analysis of the availability of AG ISF funds to cover shortfalls within the Division; and (5) Legislative suggestions to support more stable funding of legal costs within the Division.

lineitem.name: Clinical Services
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Clinical Services

lineitem.name: Department Oversight
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Department Oversight

lineitem.name: Health Care Administration
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Health Care Administration

program.name: Provider Reimbursement Information System for Medicaid
program.display: Provider Reimbursement Information System for Medicaid

lineitem.name: Integrated Health Care Services
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Integrated Health Care Services

program.name: Medicaid Home and Community Based Services
program.display: Medicaid Home and Community Based Services

program.name: Non-Medicaid Behavioral Health Treatment and Crisis Response
program.display: Non-Medicaid Behavioral Health Treatment and Crisis Response

lineitem.appintent: Under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G Chapter 6b, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services provide direct award grants of $150,000 to Fit to Recover and $100,000 to the School of Addiction Recovery in Fiscal Year 2026 for services to individuals recovering from substance use disorders.

lineitem.name: Long-Term Services & Support
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Long-Term Services Support
lineitem.appintent: Notwithstanding the intent language included in Laws of Utah 2025, Chapter 539, Item 380, the Legislature intends that under the provisions of Utah Code Annotated Title 63G, Chapter 6b, $200,000 one-time provided by Laws of Utah 2025, Chapter 539, Item 396, be utilized by the Department of Health and Human Services to support dementia care in rural areas.

lineitem.name: Public Health, Prevention, and Epidemiology
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Public Health, Prevention, and Epidemiology

program.name: State Epidemiology Informatics and Surveillance & Public Health Infrastructure
program.display: State Epidemiology Informatics and Surveillance Public Health Infrastructure

lineitem.appintent:
The Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services provide potential options to change latent tuberculosis treatment guidelines issued by the Department of Health and Human Services for individuals identified via employment and school screening to have the private healthcare system be responsible for treatment to the Social Servies Appropriations Subcommittee by June 1, 2026.
The Legislature authorizes the Department of Health and Human Services to charge the following fees in FY 2026: (1) $10,000 access to immunization records database - up to 100,000 records, (2) $15,000 access to immunization records database - 100,001-200,000 records, (3) $20,000 access to immunization records database - unlimited records, and (4) $150,000 access to immunization records database - unlimited records + customizations.
The Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report to the Social Services Appropriations Subcommittee by June 1, 2026 on possible alternatives to the $81,000 annual payment to reserve two beds for non-compliant active tuberculosis patients and feasibility of serving compliant active tuberculosis patients with other providers.

lineitem.name: Children, Youth, & Families
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Children, Youth, Families

program.name: Child Abuse Prevention and Facility Services
program.display: Child Abuse Prevention and Facility Services

program.name: Office of Coordinated Care and Regional Supports
program.display: Office of Coordinated Care and Regional Supports

lineitem.name: Office of Recovery Services
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Office of Recovery Services

lineitem.name: Juvenile Justice & Youth Services
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Juvenile Justice Youth Services

lineitem.name: Correctional Health Services
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Correctional Health Services

licat.summary: The Legislature has reviewed the following expendable funds. The Legislature authorizes the State Division of Finance to transfer amounts between funds and accounts as indicated. Outlays and expenditures from the funds or accounts to which the money is transferred may be made without further legislative action, in accordance with statutory provisions relating to the funds or accounts.

agency.name: Department of Health and Human Services
agency.display: DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES

lineitem.name: Allyson Gamble Organ Donation Contribution Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Allyson Gamble Organ Donation Contribution Fund

lineitem.name: Brain and Spinal Cord Injury Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Brain and Spinal Cord Injury Fund

lineitem.name: Maurice N. Warshaw Trust Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Maurice N. Warshaw Trust Fund

lineitem.name: Out and About Homebound Transportation Assistance Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Out and About Homebound Transportation Assistance Fund

lineitem.name: Utah State Developmental Center Long-Term Sustainability Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Utah State Developmental Center Long-Term Sustainability Fund

lineitem.name: Utah State Developmental Center Miscellaneous Donation Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Utah State Developmental Center Miscellaneous Donation Fund

lineitem.name: Utah State Developmental Center Workshop Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Utah State Developmental Center Workshop Fund

lineitem.name: Utah State Hospital Unit Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Utah State Hospital Unit Fund

lineitem.name: Mental Health Services Donation Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Mental Health Services Donation Fund

lineitem.name: Suicide Prevention and Education Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Suicide Prevention and Education Fund

lineitem.name: Licensed Provider Assessment Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Licensed Provider Assessment Fund

licat.summary: The Legislature has reviewed the following proprietary funds. Under the terms and conditions of Utah Code 63J-1-410, for any included Internal Service Fund, the Legislature approves budgets, full-time permanent positions, and capital acquisition amounts as indicated, and appropriates to the funds, as indicated, estimated revenue from rates, fees, and other charges. The Legislature authorizes the State Division of Finance to transfer amounts between funds and accounts as indicated.

agency.name: Department of Health and Human Services
agency.display: DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES

lineitem.name: Qualified Patient Enterprise Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Qualified Patient Enterprise Fund

licat.summary: The Legislature authorizes the State Division of Finance to transfer the following amounts between the following funds or accounts as indicated. Expenditures and outlays from the funds to which the money is transferred must be authorized by an appropriation.

lineitem.name: Medicaid ACA Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Medicaid ACA Fund

lineitem.name: Alternative Eligibility Account
lineitem.lidesc: Alternative Eligibility Account

licat.summary: The Legislature has reviewed proposed revenues, expenditures, fund balances, and changes in fund balances for the following fiduciary funds.

agency.name: Department of Health and Human Services
agency.display: DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES

lineitem.name: Human Services Client Trust Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Human Services Client Trust Fund

lineitem.name: Human Services ORS Support Collections
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Human Services ORS Support Collections

lineitem.name: Utah State Developmental Center Patient Account
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Utah State Developmental Center Patient Account

lineitem.name: Utah State Hospital Patient Trust Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Utah State Hospital Patient Trust Fund

sectionText.summary: The following sums of money are appropriated for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2026, and ending June 30, 2027. These are additions to amounts previously appropriated for fiscal year 2027.

licat.summary: Under the terms and conditions of Title 63J, Chapter 1, Budgetary Procedures Act, the Legislature appropriates the following sums of money from the funds or accounts indicated for the use and support of the government of the state of Utah.

agency.name: Department of Health and Human Services
agency.display: DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES

lineitem.name: Operations
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Operations
lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Operations line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Number of DHHS Audit Recommendations Unresolved After One Year (Target = 5); 2. Percent of Key Data Systems That Are Modernized, Optimized, and Integrated by 2026 (American Rescue Plan Act Project Tracking) (Target = 100%); and 3. Percent of Strategic Objectives That Are Due and Completed per Fiscal Year (Target = 80%).

lineitem.name: Clinical Services
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Clinical Services

appsrc.name: Department of Public Safety Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From Department of Public Safety Restricted Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Opioid Litigation Proceeds Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Opioid Litigation Proceeds Restricted Account

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Clinical Services line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Percent of Operational Units or Offices That Increase Their Activity Score After Participating in the Building Organizational Capacity Project (Target = 100%); 2. Percentage of Autopsy Reports Completed Within 60 Days (Target = 90%); and 3. Percentage of Turnaround Times Standards Met (Target = 90%).

lineitem.name: Department Oversight
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Department Oversight
lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Department Oversight line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Number of Days Between Criminal Record Released and Staff Determination (Target = 5) and 2. Rate of Increased Provider Compliance With Licensing Rules (Target = 5%).

lineitem.name: Health Care Administration
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Health Care Administration

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Statewide Behavioral Health Crisis Response Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Statewide Behavioral Health Crisis Response Account

appsrc.name: Ambulance Service Provider Assess Exp Rev Fund
appsrc.display: From Ambulance Service Provider Assess Exp Rev Fund

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Alternative Eligibility Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Alternative Eligibility Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Electronic Cigarette Substance and Nicotine Product Proceeds Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Electronic Cigarette Substance and Nicotine Product Proceeds Restricted Account

appsrc.name: Nursing Care Facilities Provider Assessment Fund
appsrc.display: From Nursing Care Facilities Provider Assessment Fund

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Opioid Litigation Proceeds Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Opioid Litigation Proceeds Restricted Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Tobacco Settlement Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Tobacco Settlement Account

program.name: Provider Reimbursement Information System for Medicaid
program.display: Provider Reimbursement Information System for Medicaid

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Health Care Administration line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Average Decision Time of Medicaid Medical Prior Authorizations (Target = 5); 2. Health Program Representative Customer Service Line Average Call Wait Time (Target = 2); and 3. Percent of Medicaid Members/patients/clients That Report Adequate Access to DHHS Program Services (Target = 85%).

lineitem.name: Integrated Health Care Services
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Integrated Health Care Services

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Statewide Behavioral Health Crisis Response Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Statewide Behavioral Health Crisis Response Account

appsrc.name: Ambulance Service Provider Assess Exp Rev Fund
appsrc.display: From Ambulance Service Provider Assess Exp Rev Fund

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Alternative Eligibility Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Alternative Eligibility Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Electronic Cigarette Substance and Nicotine Product Proceeds Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Electronic Cigarette Substance and Nicotine Product Proceeds Restricted Account

appsrc.name: Nursing Care Facilities Provider Assessment Fund
appsrc.display: From Nursing Care Facilities Provider Assessment Fund

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Opioid Litigation Proceeds Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Opioid Litigation Proceeds Restricted Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Tobacco Settlement Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Tobacco Settlement Account

program.name: Medicaid Home and Community Based Services
program.display: Medicaid Home and Community Based Services

program.name: Non-Medicaid Behavioral Health Treatment and Crisis Response
program.display: Non-Medicaid Behavioral Health Treatment and Crisis Response

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Integrated Health Care Services line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Annual State General Funds Saved Through Preferred Drug List (Target = $20,000,000); 2. Average Percent of Patients in Delay (Target = 19.9%); 3. Percent of Clean Claims Adjudicated by Provider Reimbursement Information System for Medicaid Within 30 Days of Submission (Target = 90%); 4. Percent of Medicaid Adult Members That Receive Services From an Integrated Health Plan or Other Integrated Model (Target = 40%); 5. Percent of Medicaid Adults and Adolescents With Major Depressive Episodes Who Receive Treatment (Target = 60.9%); 6. Percent of Medicaid Members Who Promptly Receive Outpatient Treatment After Visiting a Hospital for Mental Health Issues (Target = 59%); 7. Percentage of Adult Clients With Improved Symptoms, or Recovered, as Measured by the Adult Mental Health Outcome (Target = 45%); 8. Percentage of Individuals Who Transitioned From Intermediate Care Facilities to Community-based Services (Target = 10%); 9. Percentage of Youth Clients With Improved Symptoms, or Recovered, as Measured by the Youth Outcome Questionnaires (Target = 50%); and 10. Rates of Utahns Dying of Drug-Related Causes (Target = -1.0).

lineitem.name: Long-Term Services & Support
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Long-Term Services Support

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Division of Services for People with Disabilities Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Division of Services for People with Disabilities Restricted Account

program.name: Services for People with Disabilities Administration
program.display: Services for People with Disabilities Administration

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Long-Term Services Support line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. People Receiving Supports in Their Home or a Family Member's Home Rather Than a Residential Setting (Target = 57%); 2. Percent of Adults Who Report That Services and Supports Help Them Live a Good Life (Target = 92%); 3. Percent of Individuals Who Do Not Currently Have a Paid Job in the Community, but Would Like a Job in the Community (NCI) (Target = 44%); 4. Percent of Office of the Public Guardian (OPG) Referrals Where an Alternative to Guardianship With OPG Is Made (Target = 60%); and 5. The Percentage of APS Clients Who Accept Referrals to Community Services (Target = 60%).

lineitem.name: Public Health, Prevention, and Epidemiology
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Public Health, Prevention, and Epidemiology

appsrc.name: Gen. Fund Rest. - State Lab Drug Testing Account
appsrc.display: From Gen. Fund Rest. - State Lab Drug Testing Account

program.name: Office of Primary Care & Rural Health
program.display: Office of Primary Care Rural Health

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Public Health, Prevention, and Epidemiology line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Decrease the Percent of Utah Adults Who Report Fair or Poor General Health in Very High Health Improvement Index Areas (Target = -1%); 2. Number of Utahns Who Experience a Preventable Illness or Injury of Public Health Concern (Target = 149); and 3. Percentage of Rules, Disease Plans, and Response Plans That Are Current (Target = 75%).

lineitem.name: Children, Youth, & Families
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Children, Youth, Families

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Adult Autism Treatment Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Adult Autism Treatment Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Victim Services Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Victim Services Restricted Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Children's Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Children's Account

appsrc.name: Gen. Fund Rest. - K. Oscarson Children's Organ Transp.
appsrc.display: From Gen. Fund Rest. - K. Oscarson Children's Organ Transp.

program.name: Child Abuse Prevention and Facility Services
program.display: Child Abuse Prevention and Facility Services

program.name: Office of Coordinated Care and Regional Supports
program.display: Office of Coordinated Care and Regional Supports

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Children, Youth, Families line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Average Number of Caseworkers per Case (percent Decrease From FY22 Rate) (Target = 5); 2. Average Number of Placements per Child (moves per 1,000 Days) (Target = 4.48); 3. Case Worker Turnover Rate (Target = 22.4%); 4. Percent of Children Confirmed as Victims of Abuse or Neglect Who Experienced Repeat Maltreatment Within 12 Months (Target = 9.7%); 5. Percent of Reunification (percent Increase Over the FY21 Rate) (Target = 2%); and 6. Percent Of_children Who Demonstrated Improvement in Social-emotional_skills, Including Social Relationships. (Target = 56%).

lineitem.name: Juvenile Justice & Youth Services
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Juvenile Justice Youth Services

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Juvenile Justice Reinvestment Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Juvenile Justice Reinvestment Account

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Juvenile Justice Youth Services line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Percent of Youth During Custody Who Have Reduced Dynamic Risk (Target = 80%) and 2. Percent of Youth Who Avoid JJYS, DCFS, or Formal Probation Orders Within 90 Days of Release From the Implementation Phase of the Youth Services Plan (Target = 100%).

lineitem.name: Correctional Health Services
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Correctional Health Services
lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Correctional Health Services line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Percentage of Dental Exams Performed Within 7 Days of Admission (or Evidence of Refusal) (Target = 90%); 2. Percentage of Initial Health Assessments Completed Within 7 Days of Admission (or Evidence of Refusal) (Target = 90%); 3. Percentage of Inmates Failing to Keep Appointments (Target = 10%); and 4. Percentage of Mental Health Screenings Completed Within 14 Days of Admission (Target = 90%).

licat.summary: The Legislature has reviewed the following expendable funds. The Legislature authorizes the State Division of Finance to transfer amounts between funds and accounts as indicated. Outlays and expenditures from the funds or accounts to which the money is transferred may be made without further legislative action, in accordance with statutory provisions relating to the funds or accounts.

agency.name: Department of Health and Human Services
agency.display: DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES

lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Allyson Gamble Organ Donation Contribution Fund

lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Utah State Developmental Center Long-Term Sustainability Fund

lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Utah State Developmental Center Workshop Fund

program.name: Utah State Developmental Center Workshop Fund
program.display: Utah State Developmental Center Workshop Fund

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Utah State Developmental Center Workshop Fund line item performance measure for FY 2027: Internal Reviews of the Utah State Developmental Center Workshop Fund (Target = 1).

lineitem.name: Utah State Hospital Unit Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Utah State Hospital Unit Fund
lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Utah State Hospital Unit Fund line item performance measure for FY 2027: Internal Reviews of the Utah State Hospital Unit Fund (Target = 1).

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Mental Health Services Donation Fund line item performance measure for FY 2027: Number of Internal Reviews Completed for Compliance With Statute, Federal Regulations, and Other Requirements (Target = 1).

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Suicide Prevention and Education Fund line item performance measure for FY 2027: Number of Internal Reviews Completed for Compliance With Statute, Federal Regulations, and Other Requirements (Target = 1).

lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Licensed Provider Assessment Fund

licat.summary: The Legislature has reviewed the following proprietary funds. Under the terms and conditions of Utah Code 63J-1-410, for any included Internal Service Fund, the Legislature approves budgets, full-time permanent positions, and capital acquisition amounts as indicated, and appropriates to the funds, as indicated, estimated revenue from rates, fees, and other charges. The Legislature authorizes the State Division of Finance to transfer amounts between funds and accounts as indicated.

sectionText.summary: The following sums of money are appropriated for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2026, and ending June 30, 2027. These are additions to amounts previously appropriated for fiscal year 2027.

licat.summary: Under the terms and conditions of Title 63J, Chapter 1, Budgetary Procedures Act, the Legislature appropriates the following sums of money from the funds or accounts indicated for the use and support of the government of the state of Utah.

agency.name: Department of Health and Human Services
agency.display: DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES

lineitem.name: Operations

program.name: State Epidemiology Informatics and Surveillance & Public Health Infrastructure
program.display: State Epidemiology Informatics and Surveillance Public Health Infrastructure

lineitem.appintent: The Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services and local health departments shall report by October 1, 2026 to the Social Services Appropriations Subcommittee on the feasibility of billing public health services for managing tuberculosis cases to insurance. The report shall include estimates of implementation costs and potential revenue.

lineitem.name: Office of Recovery Services
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Health and Human Services - Office of Recovery Services
lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Health and Human Services report on the following Office of Recovery Services line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Current Support Collection Rates (Target = 65%); 2. Medical Coverage for Children (Target = 65%); and 3. Overall Cost Effectiveness of the Office of Recovery Services (Target = 4.5%).

subsection.amend: Except as provided in Subsection (2), this bill takes effect July 1, 2026.
The actions affecting Section 1 (Effective upon governor's approval) take effect:
except as provided in Subsection (2)(b), May 6, 2026; or
if approved by two-thirds of all members elected to each house:
without the governor's signature, the day following the constitutional time limit of Utah Constitution, Article VII, Section 8; or
in the case of a veto, the date of veto override.
